$comment: ELF64 EXECUTABLE (ET_EXEC) for x86_64 Linux. Second ELF schema (plan 14 LK1 cycle 2, 2026-05-30). Consumed by `src/link/format/elf.cpp` via the format-blind linker engine. Differs from `elf64-x86_64-linux.format.json` (the .o schema) in: `elf.type = exec`, sections carry `virtualAddress` (used as sh_addr), top-level `entryPoint` names the entry symbol (resolved against AssembledModule at link time). Cycle-2 scope: self-contained modules (zero relocations). Modules with relocations fail loud ŌĆö the cross-reference applier lives at LK6 (D-LK4-5 + D-LK4-6 + D-LK1-3).
$dataModelComment: FC3 c1: 64-bit linux/darwin are LP64 (int 32, long 64, ptr 64). Consumed by the semantic tier (buildCuMir threads format.dataModel() into analyze()): c-subset long binds I64 here. REQUIRED closed-enum field; the loader rejects a missing or unknown value (a silent default would bake wrong widths).
$bitFieldStrategyComment: D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD-ABI-EXACT: SysV/GNU LSB-first bit-field packing (byte-ABI-exact to gcc). OS/format-determined (x86_64 serves both ELF and PE), so on the format not the target. See elf64-x86_64-linux for the full rule.
$entryPointComment: Empty entryPoint defaults the walker to module.functions[0] (cycle-2 convention). Once D-LK1-1 closes (real symbol-name thread through HIRŌåÆLIRŌåÆAssembledFunction), this field will name the entry symbol (e.g. '_start' for Linux/glibc, 'main' for hand-rolled execs) and the walker will resolve it by name. When D-LK10-ENTRY Slice C lands, the linker prepends a synthetic `_start` trampoline as module.functions[0] and the schema's entryPoint names the USER fn (the trampoline's call target).
$externCallDispatchComment: ELF resolves imports through the PLT: the linker synthesizes a PLT stub per extern (jmp through the GOT slot) and points the extern symbol's VA at the STUB, so the call site is a PLAIN DIRECT call (E8 disp32 = call rel32 to the stub). An indirect-slot (FF 15) call site would dereference the stub's CODE bytes as a pointer -> SIGSEGV. D-FFI-EXTERN-CALL-DISPATCH.
$dataImportBindingComment: c84 (D-LK-EXTERN-DATA-IMPORT): extern library DATA objects (libc stdout/stderr/stdin) bind via ELF COPY RELOCATIONS ŌĆö the standard glibc non-PIE ET_EXEC mechanism: the exec reserves a correctly-sized+aligned .bss slot per imported object, exports the symbol as a DEFINED OBJECT (st_size = the layout-derived object size) at that slot, and emits one R_X86_64_COPY (5) in .rela.dyn; ld-linux memcpy's the library's object into the slot before entry and ALL images (incl. libc itself, by interposition) reference the exec's copy. Zero new instruction encodings ŌĆö module code reaches the object through the normal GlobalAddr path. A format WITHOUT this field rejects data imports loud at the linker's pre-walker gate (a data symbol bound through the function-import machinery would read jump-stub bytes as the object's value). The PIE-forward GOT-indirect model is a future member.
$supportedDataSectionsComment: D-LK1-ELF-EXEC-DATA-SECTIONS (rodata) + D-LK4-DATA-PRODUCER (writable `.data` + zero-fill `.bss`) + D-CSUBSET-THREAD-LOCAL (thread-local `.tdata`/`.tbss`, TLS C1): the ELF ET_EXEC walker emits `.rodata` (read-only, R+X PT_LOAD), `.data` (mutable initialized globals, R+W PT_LOAD), `.bss` (zero-init globals, R+W PT_LOAD, NOBITS), `.tdata` (initialized thread-local template, opens the R+W PT_LOAD, covered by PT_TLS), and `.tbss` (zero-fill thread-local extent, NOBITS, PT_TLS memsz only ŌĆö NO file/segment footprint) from `AssembledModule.dataItems` per the item's `section`. The linker's pre-walker gate (linker.cpp) consults this set per-item. Cross-format agnosticism: this is the SAME field PE/Mach-O exec formats declare; the gate has zero format-name branches. tdata/tbss here is THE x86_64-linux opt-in ŌĆö arm64-linux opts in at TLS C2, pe64 at C3, Mach-O at C4; until then their walkers ALSO carry a K_FormatLacksThreadLocalSupport belt.
$tlsAccessComment: TLS C1 (D-CSUBSET-THREAD-LOCAL): how code reaches a thread-local object's per-thread copy on this leg. local-exec = the ELF static-TLS model for a non-PIE executable: glibc's ld.so processes PT_TLS for every thread (the CSU runs before main on this ALWAYS-dynamic arm), sets fs.base, and stores the thread pointer's own address at fs:[0] (the tcbhead self-pointer) ŌĆö so `mov r, fs:[0]` (segmentPrefixByte 100 = 0x64 fs-override, baseDisplacement 0) reads tp, and a symbol's address is tp + tpoff(sym) with tpoff a LINK-TIME constant (the tls-tpoff32 reloc; x86_64 Variant II ŌćÆ negative). MIRŌåÆLIR reads this block to emit the tlsbase + lea pair; a format WITHOUT the block fails loud K_FormatLacksThreadLocalSupport on the first thread-local access (pe64/macho omit it until their TLS cycles). PE later declares the SAME x86 primitive with config-only differences: model pe-indexed, 101 = 0x65 gs, baseDisplacement 0x58 (the TEB ThreadLocalStoragePointer).
$artifactProfilesComment: Profiles this format SERVES (plan 06 AP3) ŌĆö a runnable console executable ŌćÆ cli. The format-side twin of the language's artifactProfiles[] (AP1): the driver fails loud (D_ArtifactProfileFormatMismatch) if a project's profile isn't in this set. A future shared-library format would declare ["lib"] with ZERO gate-code change ŌĆö the gate is a generic set-membership, never a format-name branch.
$processExitComment: STDIO-FLUSH-AT-EXIT (2026-06-09): terminate via libc `exit(3)`, NOT the raw `exit_group(2)` syscall. The C standard says returning from `main` ŌēĪ calling `exit(3)`, which flushes line-/full-buffered stdio + runs `atexit` handlers before the process dies. The prior raw `exit_group` syscall skipped that flush, so a program that did `puts("hello")` then returned exited cleanly but with stdout still buffered (empty on a pipe). By-name-import of libc `exit` makes the trampoline emit a DIRECT `call` to the PLT stub for `exit` (per externCallDispatch=direct-plt), exactly as a user-level FFI call (`puts`) lowers ŌĆö so `exit` flushes stdout, runs atexit, then calls `_exit`/`exit_group` itself. This routes EVERY ELF program through the dynamic writer (libc.so.6 becomes a DT_NEEDED, a PLT stub + GOT slot is synthesized for `exit`, ld-linux resolves it) ŌĆö the SAME machinery `extern_call_elf` already uses; a program whose ONLY extern is this `exit` works because it is identical in shape to the FFI case. importMangledName is the canonical C name `exit` (ELF adds no leading underscore ŌĆö cFormatAddsLeadingUnderscore(Elf)=false). Uniform with macho (libSystem `exit`) + pe (kernel32 `ExitProcess`, which flushes via msvcrt's DLL-detach handler): all three terminate through a libc-level, stdio-flushing primitive.
$entryCallingConventionComment: SysV AMD64 calling convention ŌĆö status (user fn return value in rax) is moved into argGprs[0] = rdi before SYSCALL. Read via `target.callingConventionByName("sysv_amd64")` at trampoline-build time.
$processArgsComment: c88 (D-RUNTIME-MAIN-ARGC-ARGV): program-entry argc/argv materialization. SysV AMD64 psABI ┬¦3.4.1 ŌĆö at the process entry point the kernel/loader leaves argc (a machine word) at [rsp+0] and the NULL-terminated argv pointer vector IN PLACE starting at [rsp+8] (envp follows argv's NULL; not materialized ŌĆö the c-subset entry signature is (int, char**)). The trampoline emits `mov rdi, [rsp+0]` (argc ŌåÆ argGprs[0]) + `lea rsi, [rsp+8]` (the vector's ADDRESS ŌåÆ argGprs[1]) BEFORE any SP adjustment and before `call main`. A main(void) simply ignores the two registers (C-legal) ŌĆö signature-independent setup. Without this block, main(argc,argv) reads process-entry register garbage (the c87 sqlite3 shell crash: argc=846361312). Destination registers come from entryCallingConvention.argGprs[0..1] ŌĆö never declared here (single source of truth).

elf.$comment: Elf64_Ehdr identity for an executable. type = ET_EXEC (2). class = ELFCLASS64; data = ELFDATA2LSB; osabi = sysv; machine = EM_X86_64 (62). pageAlign = 4096 (Linux x86_64 4 KB pages ŌĆö kernel's PT_LOAD congruence requires p_vaddr % p_align == p_offset % p_align). ARM64 Linux on Apple Silicon / 64K configurations declares 16384 / 65536 in its own schema (D-LK6-3).
elf.$bindNowComment: Eager binding ŌĆö the v1 stance per plan 14 ┬¦5 risk row. Emits DT_FLAGS_1 = DF_1_NOW so dyld resolves every GOT slot at load via R_X86_64_GLOB_DAT in .rela.dyn. Lazy upgrade (.rela.plt + R_X86_64_JUMP_SLOT + 16-byte PLT0 resolver-trampoline) anchored at D-LK6-11; until then, the walker fails loud on bindNow=false.

sections.$comment: Executable code section. virtualAddress = 0x401000 ŌĆö conventional Linux x86_64 base (0x400000) + 0x1000 to leave the first page for the Ehdr and program header table loaded alongside `.text` in the same PT_LOAD. This is a convention, not a kernel hard requirement: any page-aligned VA works as long as `p_vaddr % p_align == p_offset % p_align`. sh_addr is set to this; e_entry derives from this + entry-function offset. sh_flags = SHF_ALLOC | SHF_EXECINSTR (6); sh_addralign = 16 (x86_64 psABI).
sections.name: .text

sections.$comment: Read-only initialized data (string literals, const/primitive globals). sh_type=SHT_PROGBITS(1); sh_flags=SHF_ALLOC(2) ŌĆö readable, NOT writable/executable (it is folded into the R+X text PT_LOAD this cycle, which is strictly less permissive than writable; W^X preserved). sh_addralign=8. virtualAddress=0 ŌĆö the writer COMPUTES the rodata VA contiguously after .text (secText.virtualAddress + alignUp(text.size, 8)); a fixed VA would collide with .text. D-LK1-ELF-EXEC-DATA-SECTIONS.
sections.name: .rodata

sections.$comment: Writable initialized data (mutable primitive/aggregate globals). sh_type=SHT_PROGBITS(1); sh_flags=SHF_ALLOC|SHF_WRITE(3) ŌĆö readable + WRITABLE (a store into it must not fault). Placed in a SEPARATE R+W PT_LOAD from the R+X text/rodata so mutable data never shares a page with code (W^X). sh_addralign=8. virtualAddress=0 ŌĆö the writer COMPUTES the `.data` VA (page-aligned writable segment after the read-only sections). D-LK4-DATA-PRODUCER.
sections.name: .data

sections.$comment: Zero-initialized data (tentative globals `int g;`). sh_type=SHT_NOBITS(8) ŌĆö occupies NO file bytes; the loader zero-fills sh_size bytes at the section VA. sh_flags=SHF_ALLOC|SHF_WRITE(3) ŌĆö readable + writable. Contributes to the R+W PT_LOAD's p_memsz but NOT its p_filesz. sh_addralign=8. virtualAddress=0 ŌĆö the writer COMPUTES the `.bss` VA (last in the writable segment). D-LK4-DATA-PRODUCER.
sections.name: .bss

sections.$comment: Initialized THREAD-LOCAL template data (`thread_local int g = 7;` ŌĆö D-CSUBSET-THREAD-LOCAL, TLS C1). sh_type=SHT_PROGBITS(1); sh_flags=SHF_WRITE|SHF_ALLOC|SHF_TLS(0x403=1027) ŌĆö the TLS bit is what tells inspectors (readelf/lldb) these bytes are a per-thread TEMPLATE, not process data. The walker places it as the FIRST file-backed member of the R+W PT_LOAD #2 and points PT_TLS at it (p_offset/p_vaddr=here, p_filesz=this section, p_memsz=+tbss); glibc's ld.so copies the template into every thread's static TLS block (main thread included ŌĆö the CSU runs before main on the always-dynamic ELF arm). sh_addralign floor 1 ŌĆö MEMBER-DRIVEN (the walker raises to the strictest member alignment, which also becomes PT_TLS p_align). Deliberately NOT the .data-style floor 8: PT_TLS p_align feeds the tpoff formulas (tpoff = offset - alignUp(memsz, p_align) on Variant II) and glibc's per-thread block placement, so a gratuitous floor would inflate every thread's block + diverge from the gcc-witnessed offsets (gcc: thread_local int -> sh_addralign 4, p_align 4). virtualAddress=0 ŌĆö computed.
sections.name: .tdata

sections.$comment: Zero-fill THREAD-LOCAL template extent (`thread_local int g;` ŌĆö D-CSUBSET-THREAD-LOCAL, TLS C1). sh_type=SHT_NOBITS(8); sh_flags=SHF_WRITE|SHF_ALLOC|SHF_TLS(0x403=1027). Unlike `.bss` it occupies NO file bytes AND NO PT_LOAD memory ŌĆö its span lives ONLY in PT_TLS p_memsz (the loader allocates + zeroes it per thread); sh_addr/sh_offset are the nominal just-past-.tdata values (the gcc overlap convention for NOBITS TLS). sh_addralign floor 1 ŌĆö member-driven, same rationale as .tdata. virtualAddress=0 ŌĆö computed.
sections.name: .tbss

sections.$comment: Symbol table ŌĆö even in ET_EXEC files, .symtab is conventional for debugger support. sh_type = SHT_SYMTAB (2); sh_addralign = 8; sh_entsize = 24 (Elf64_Sym).
sections.name: .symtab

sections.$comment: Symbol-name string table. sh_type = SHT_STRTAB (3); addrAlign = 1.
sections.name: .strtab

sections.$comment: Section-header string table. sh_type = SHT_STRTAB (3); addrAlign = 1.
sections.name: .shstrtab

relocations.$comment: Relocation kinds are declared so the linker engine's cross-reference unifier (plan 13 ┬¦2.6) accepts any relocation tag the assembler stamps. In ET_EXEC cycle 2, the walker rejects modules whose AssembledFunctions carry ANY relocation entries (cycle scope: self-contained only). Resolved at LK6 D-LK4-5/D-LK4-6 + plan 14 D-LK1-3.
relocations.name: R_X86_64_PC32

relocations.$comment: D-CSUBSET-THREAD-LOCAL (TLS C1): the local-exec thread-pointer-offset fixup ŌĆö the disp32 of `lea r, [tp + tpoff32(sym)]`. kind=4 matches the target schema's `tls-tpoff32` row (Linear, width 4, absolute, tls:true); the walker patches the 4 bytes from its tpoff-valued symbolVa entry, so the exec image carries NO runtime reloc for it (link-time constant). nativeId = R_X86_64_TPOFF32 (23) ŌĆö only meaningful for a future ET_REL emission of this kind, but the row's existence is what satisfies the linker's pre-walker cross-reference unifier + the walker's in-arm kind check.
relocations.name: R_X86_64_TPOFF32
